title: Spectrophotometric Quantification of Betacyanins in Plant Tissue Produced from the RUBY Reporter Gene
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ct52s","text":"RUBY is a visual reporter gene that produces beatnin, a betacyanin that appears red to violet in colour (He et al., 2020). To assay betanin (and other betalains) a spectrophotometric approach has been employed, as seen in Stintzing et al. (2003) and Grützner et al. (2021).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"ahqcd","text":"- 2 ml micro-centrifuge tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"42htc","text":"- Mini pestle","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1fk5","text":"- Methanol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1pbem","text":"- Ascorbic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1prng","text":"- Formic acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bvsch","text":"- Cuvettes/96 well plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dnl61","text":"- Spectrophotometer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Take 20-50 mg of frozen tissue and add to 2 ml tubes. Keep on ice.
Grind down tissue using plastic micro-pestle and add methanol buffer (50% methanol, 1 mM ascorbic acid, 0.5% formic acid) at 10% w/v - i.e. for 35 mg of tissue add 350 ul methanol buffer.

Dilute samples with mili-Q so that absorption values fall within 0.8 - 1.0 (as previously calculated by Stintzing et al. 2003). Grützner et al. (2021) found that a 12 fold dilution performed well but this could differ.
Put cuvettes or 96 well plates into spectophotometer and read absorbtion at 538 nm (Stintzing et al. 2003).

Using the formula BC = (A * DF * MW * 1000) / ( ε x L) calculate the concentration of betacyanins. BC = betanin content (mg/L) A = absorbance (538 nm) DF = dilution factor MW = molecular weight (550.47 g/mol) L = cuvette path length ε = molar extinction coefficient (60, 000 L/(mool cm)
Punch out leaf discs (as many as possible in the target area) of areas expressing betanin.
Put leaf discs in 2 ml micro-centrifuge tubes and snap freeze in liquid nitrogen then store in -80 freezer until required.
creator: Matthew Morgan
